reviews.review_description:
When FTL launched the Infiniverse section of the Multiverse app in December 2021, they promised major features and told paying users they were “pioneers” and “investors” who would earn significant financial returns.

Within six months, FTL shifted focus away from the Infiniverse. They created multiple companies, tried to raise funds for four new games, and secured investment for one of them — The Flip.

By January 2023, FTL had stopped updating the Multiverse app and went silent with the Infiniverse community for 19 months.

They also changed theeir ToS to state users owned nothing regardless of how much they spent, and incentivised certain users to leave fake five‑star reviews on the Meta Store.

When development resumed on the Infiniverse mid‑2024, FTL only delivered low‑effort, trivial features while abandoning most of the promises made in 2022–23. Long‑standing bugs from 2022 were never fixed. FTL deleted evidence of their misleading claims for the two years prior, and anyone who complained was permanently banned from their social channels.

Yesterday, both Multiverse and The Flip were shut down, leaving many Infiniverse users who spent thousands of dollars — believing it was a financial investment — with nothing.

FTL has now removed all evidence of their misconduct, leaving frustrated users with no recourse for the money they lost.

My Experience in the Multiverse App
I absolutely love the open-world concept of the Multiverse — it’s truly one of a kind! You can explore by foot, soar through the skies with a jetpack, or instantly travel through portals to your destination of choice. Over the last 6 months or so we been provided more assets giving us more freedom and creativity to create unique worlds. 
One of the best parts about this app is how accessible the developers are — they’re present in-game, on Discord, Facebook, or email them directly. I feel that level of engagement really sets Multiverse apart from other virtual platforms.
During my time here, I’ve been able to build Women’s World , a thriving community designed to empower, connect, and redefine what’s possible. We’re now approaching our 4th Annual Women’s World Retreat, and through this platform, I’ve also created:
* Sista’s Book Club – a space for inspiring conversations and connection
* MetaFit Gym – fitness and wellness reimagined in VR
* FEM Global Community – uniting female entrepreneurs across the metaverse
* Diversity of Gifts – a community where individuals share their talents and creativity
* Beautiful immersive spaces like Birch Bay Mountain Resort & Winery, The Enchanted Pavilion, Hidden Palm Ravine, and the Meta Legacy Coin community. 
The sky’s truly the limit for what’s possible here. Even during slower periods, I’ve used the time to innovate and create — and the arrival of new assets and updates keeps the experience fresh and exciting. Watching creators bring their imaginations to life in real time is inspiring.
Plug for Women’s World!�Join us for the 4th Annual Women’s World Metaverse Retreat, happening November 21–23, 2025 right here in the Multiverse (and in Meta Horizons). The retreat runs 24 hours a day on Friday and Saturday, and 9 AM–6 PM on Sunday. You’ll get to experience the incredible creativity of our community and see firsthand why Multiverse is a special place.
I would say this is more than an app — it’s a world where innovation, community, and imagination come alive. I have already met in real life over 10 people across the world! The app is really about what you make it. When you come in find me. You’ll receive a nice home apartment when you come into the game. The second one is in me! I am Women’s World in the app.

reviews.review_title: Why Now Is the Time to Revisit t
reviews.review_description:
The Multiverse app on Meta Quest has always been a unique corner of social VR—a space that’s part digital art gallery, part shopping mall, part sci-fi sandbox. But what’s happening now in the Multiverse deserves a second look, especially for creators, brand builders, and anyone curious about the future of AI-powered interaction in virtual worlds.

Custom Apartments & Storefronts: Your Space, Your Style

First things first—the customization options in Multiverse are on another level. Whether you’re creating a personal retreat, a retail experience, or an entire branded universe, the tools available allow you to design with complete creative control.
	•	Apartments can be decorated with wall art, videos, and textures to reflect your personal aesthetic. It’s like stepping into your imagination and setting up shop.
	•	Storefronts offer creators and entrepreneurs a way to visually represent their brand in 3D. Add portals to your website, videos, NFT displays, product previews, and more. Each storefront becomes a fully immersive, explorable extension of your identity or mission.

But what truly sets the Multiverse apart in 2025 is its growing AI ecosystem.

AI Agents: The Future of Virtual Interaction Is Here

Multiverse now includes AI agents that users can customize, train, and deploy—and it’s a game-changer.

These AI agents aren’t just passive greeters. You can:
	•	Tailor their appearance, tone, and personality to match your brand or vibe.
	•	Assign them to your storefront as virtual salespeople, able to greet visitors, provide information, recommend products, or guide users through your content.
	•	Train them to represent you and your vision—whether that’s selling art, teaching visitors about your project, or offering a warm welcome to new arrivals.

But it gets better…

AI Agent Collaboration & Training Systems

Multiverse now supports the training of AI agents to work together as a team within your metaverse. That means:
	•	Your AI greeter can direct users to your AI product expert.
	•	Your tutorial guide can onboard new users and hand them off to your marketing agent.
	•	You can train your agents to train others—creating a self-sustaining system for growth and engagement.

Whether you’re building a business, a fandom, or an artistic movement inside the Multiverse, you now have the tools to amplify your vision with virtual employees that actually understand what you’re trying to do.

A Community That Listens

If you’re wondering whether the developers are keeping up with user feedback—the answer is a resounding yes. On Discord, the Multiverse devs are actively involved in conversations, answering questions, sharing sneak peeks, and implementing community-suggested features.

You’re not just downloading an app—you’re joining a live, evolving platform shaped by its users.

Final Thoughts: It’s Time to Come Back

If you haven’t visited the Multiverse in a while, now’s the perfect time to dive back in.

With expanded customization, powerful AI integration, and a developer team that’s genuinely building toward the future of virtual interaction, the Multiverse is turning into more than just a VR world—it’s becoming a platform for identity, storytelling, and digital entrepreneurship.

Download the app. Explore. Customize. Talk to your AI agents.
And most importantly—see what’s changed. The future’s already being built. You just have to step inside.

reviews.review_description:
If you're looking to invest in the metaverse this is not the place. Since it’s possible to buy property for $ in the in-app world “Infiniverse” you might be tempted to do so, but after two years in pre-Beta (a nice word for Alpha) you still can’t cash out and there are no ongoing dev projects to make that happen. Buying property is more an in-app purchase for fun (although I’m not entirely sure what the “fun” part is) than a reasonable financial investment.

Just hanging out in the Infiniverse can be fun – there are a lot of nice people (if you can find them) and the users arrange some pretty cool activities (if you can find them) – but don’t expect graphic marvels or mind-blowing 3D experiences: it’s all more or less repetative low-poly and the VR tech is still on the budget side.

The part of Multiverse that is not Infiniverse is predominantly a pretty nice space exhibition. It’s well worth a visit and completely free.

In conclusion: Avoid spending more than pocket money, and don’t expect too much.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your honest response and advice, and we agree that all investments should be made with caution. Platform guidelines mean that "cashing out" is not possible but we are actively working on ways to improve opportunities for businesses.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts and experiences with Multiverse. We truly value the feedback from our community, and it's disheartening to hear that your recent experiences have not met your initial expectations. We understand your concerns and would like to address the points you've raised.

Firstly, we're glad to hear that you've met some great people through our platform. Fostering a positive and engaging community has always been one of our core values, and we're happy that aspect has been a highlight for you.

Regarding the development of the game, we sincerely apologize if it seems progress has stalled. The journey of innovation and development in such a dynamic field as ours is filled with challenges and learning curves. We assure you that our dedication to Multiverse remains steadfast. Our team is continuously working behind the scenes on updates and improvements, even if these efforts are not always immediately visible to our users.

We understand the frustration that can come from changes in the app's dynamics, especially concerning property drops and the resale market. The digital landscape is constantly evolving, and we strive to adapt in ways that balance new opportunities with the interests of our community. Our intention is never to dangle "carrots" but to create engaging and sustainable growth for our platform and its users.

Your comment regarding our attention being diverted to other apps concerns us, and we want to clarify that our team is fully committed to the development and success of Multiverse. While we do explore other projects to ensure the longevity and financial stability of our operations, Multiverse remains a priority, and we're passionate about its future.

We believe in transparent communication and are here to discuss any specific issues or suggestions you may have to improve your experience. We're committed to regaining your trust and demonstrating our dedication to the Multiverse community.

Firstly, we understand the confusion and frustration that can arise from not having a clear guide on how to navigate events within our platform. Many of our events are user-created, and some are 'self guided'. We encourage all our users to explore our FAQ section before making purchases or participating in events. This section provides valuable information on how our platform operates, including details about property management and event participation.

Regarding the purchase and development of properties, it's important to note that our platform operates on a dynamic growth model. This means that when you invest in a property, it's part of a living, evolving virtual world where buildings can grow, and new developments can occur. This dynamic is essential for creating a vibrant and ever-changing environment, which in turn can increase the value of properties over time. Your property doesn't change its address; rather, it evolves within its existing location, rising up with growth. You can always find your properties conveniently listed under the "Me" and "My Properties" sections in your wrist menu.

We also want to address your point about Meta coins. While it's true that they are not directly convertible to cash, they play a crucial role in our virtual economy, allowing users to trade, invest, and grow their virtual assets within our ecosystem.

We hope this response clarifies some of your concerns and demonstrates our commitment to improving your experience. Joining our Discord and reviewing the FAQ and links, as you mentioned, are great ways to stay informed and get the most out of our platform. If you have further questions or need assistance, please don't hesitate to reach out to us.

reviews.review_description: This app is simply a scam. Yet the devs keep promising the new users the same.  3 years going and it’s still in Pre-beta with zero signs of growth. Meanwhile the devs are getting rich off the innocence if new users while falsely promoting the product.  What they are doing is criminal at least.

developer_response.body:
We are truly disheartened to hear about your negative perception of Multiverse, and we take your accusations very seriously. Our team is committed to transparency, integrity, and the continuous development of our platform, and we would like to address the points you've raised.

First and foremost, we want to clarify that Multiverse is built with a vision of exploring the potential of web3 technology and creating a vibrant, user-driven economy. We understand that the journey of innovation, especially in emerging technologies, can be complex and challenging. Our intention has always been to develop a platform that offers real value and opportunities for our users, rather than misleading anyone about the potential returns on investment.

It's important to note that the development of groundbreaking platforms often takes time, and being in pre-beta for an extended period is not uncommon in the tech industry, especially for projects that aim to leverage new technologies like web3. Our goal has been to lay a solid and sustainable foundation for the platform, even if it means a longer development phase than initially anticipated.

We acknowledge that some decisions made in the early stages of development may not have fully aligned with our community's expectations. We are continually learning and adapting, taking feedback from our users to heart to steer the platform towards a more promising future.

Regarding the concerns about our team's financial practices, we assure you that our primary focus is on investing in the platform's growth and our community. Any profits are reinvested into the development, security, and expansion of Multiverse to ensure its longevity and success. We believe in the potential of our platform and are dedicated to making it a meaningful and rewarding experience for everyone involved.

We are always here to engage in open and honest dialogue with our users. We encourage you to reach out to us directly with any specific concerns or questions you may have, so we can address them more thoroughly. Our team is available through [contact information], and we welcome the opportunity to discuss how we can regain your trust and better demonstrate our commitment to the Multiverse community.

Thank you for sharing your feedback, and please know that we are dedicated to continuous improvement and the ethical development of our platform. We hope to have the chance to change your perspective and show the true value and potential of Multiverse in the time to come.

reviews.review_description:
My original review was positive because they gave everyone a chance to win a storefront if they wrote good reviews. But reviews are meant to be honest, so I’m changing my tune.
  There’s really not much to do in this app. The zombie game is not good. The planetarium is alright. Then there is the Infiniverse. 
I spent more than I should have in there. I felt like I was investing in something with no clear plan on where the app was heading. I know I didn’t spend nearly as much as many of the other “investors”, but I still feel regrets about spending anything at all. 
I think what made me think it was an investment was because they sell storefronts. One problem is that there is barely any traffic in there to see the storefront. Another problem is that a person could spend a good $100-700+ on one of those, and if one decides they want to take a break from the subscription (because the app is boring as heck) they lose that expensive storefront.  They should be able to keep it, being so expensive. 
You can’t even get videos to work without spending at least $10 a month. 
So no. It’s not necessarily a scam, but it has predatory energy in a way. 
They put a special statue in my district for Halloween time, and that was nice of them, but I cannot justify spending any more money on this app. So I’m gonna lose that storefront that I already spent over $200 on.

developer_response.body:
Our team is constantly working on developing new and engaging features for the app, including enhancing the zombie game and expanding the offerings within the planetarium and the Infiniverse. Your feedback is invaluable to us as it helps us identify areas for improvement.

We hear your concerns about the subscription model and the loss of the storefront upon taking a break from the app. While we provide ways to market and enhance store traffic to support our storefront owners, we realize not everyone wants to utilize this feature. 

We're glad you liked the special statue we added for Halloween. It's gestures like these that we hope can enhance your experience, even in small ways.

Please know that we are here to support you and are keen on making things right. We would love the opportunity to discuss this further with you and explore how we can improve your experience with our app.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback on Multiverse. We understand that finding other users to interact with is a crucial part of the experience, and we’re here to assist you in making the most out of our expansive world.

We’d like to remind you that Multiverse operates in unique shards, each hosting up to 16 users at a time. This design ensures a smooth and stable experience for everyone, but it also means that players might be spread across different shards in our vast virtual world. To enhance your interaction with other users, we have a couple of features you might find helpful:

Wrist Menu - People Tab: Check your wrist menu and navigate to the "People" tab. Here, you’ll find a list of users currently in your immediate shard. This is a great way to see who’s around you and initiate interactions.

Wrist Menu - Friends Tab: If you’ve befriended other users in the app, you can locate them through the "Friends" tab in your wrist menu. This feature allows you to connect with your friends even if they are in different shards.

We encourage you to explore these options to enhance your experience in Multiverse. Our world is rich and diverse, and sometimes it might require a bit of exploration to find other adventurers like yourself.

Additionally, we're constantly working on updates and events to bring the community together and add to the liveliness of Multiverse. Keep an eye on our updates for new features and community events that are designed to foster interaction and fun.

Your experience and satisfaction with Multiverse are important to us. If you have any more questions or need assistance navigating the app, please don’t hesitate to contact our support team at support@ftl.ltd. We’re here to help you have the best experience possible.

Thank you for being a part of Multiverse. We hope you’ll continue to explore and connect in our ever-growing virtual world.

reviews.review_title: Game is a Scam Profiting Off Players
reviews.review_description:
Just let it be known this app has been out for maybe 3 years now.

What is the Multiverse?: Well at first it was a planetary app to do star gazing and gather other planetary information.
(Shady Activities)
 Some time after they were placed in the META Store they switched up entirely and made this "Multiverse" Where you Pay in game money for all these "Apartments" that were going to make everyone potential money in the future based of land/property selling and buying. Businesses being brought into the app. They preach how there were 100's of thousands of players in the app and BIG Businesses When there was not..
The first year they claimed they sold over 200,000 apartments, then opted to open storefronts (where one could link a real business website and generate traffic from inside the app) and selling them from anywhere from $90 to $700. Once you bought store property you were forced to pay a monthly fee anywhere from $10 to $100 a month. 
After they did all this and got there monthly residual income they disappeared for a year and a half. Leaving all these people to continue to pay this monthly fee because if you didn't all your store properties purchases would disappear after you paid hundred some even thousands.

They took advantage of this and used that monthly residual income money to create an entirely different game while they made no updates to the application.

After months of people leaving there monthly plans and giving up on the app they decided to come back and offer yet again more products to purchase. Now there having "Events" where players who can advertise the most receive in game perks. Even going to the extent of having players write/review and like there own reviews. "Who ever can get the most review likes" scenarios to draw in new players and have some type of proof to match there fake numbers they give out. 
It's a SCAM stay Away!
Only people left in this app are people who spent thousands and don't want to abandon the ship. Pyramid Scheme.

reviews.review_description:
pyr·a·mid scheme
/ˈpirəˌmid skēm/
noun
a form of investment (illegal in the US and elsewhere) in which each paying participant recruits two further participants, with returns being given to early participants using money contributed by later ones.    (Only people that got in early in this game  own land  now land cost 1000,00 real money!  The rest of the people  can only buy expensive apartments from the people that own land.  The people playing this game think it’s an  investment  in digital  real estate  that will pay off when  the infiniverse explodes in popularity  at the end pre beta . The problem is on any given night the is only the same few builders in there making the same clone apartment buildings .  There are no people in the streets,  and nothing to do  for people visiting.  I can’t see this exploding in popularity when only the people at the top  own everything.  The rest can only buy an apartment. You can’t even customize your apartment beyond placing pictures on the wall.  Unfortunately the game got me for about $300.00  ( a learning lesson)  If you want to have fun building and socializing with tons of people, and exploring thousands of world creations  by users all for free then go to Horizon worlds. This probably won’t get posted. But I will post it in other places so hopefully more people won’t get sucked in to this pyramid scheme. Shame on you dev s for giving people get rich quick dreams  and giving them back an occasional trinket to keep them engaged.  People are spending thousands of dollars  in this game and the only ones making money are the devs.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share your concerns and feedback regarding your experience with Multiverse. We value your opinion and want to address the points you raised regarding the nature of the app, its financial model, and your perception of it being a pyramid scheme.

First and foremost, we want to clarify that Multiverse is not a pyramid scheme. A pyramid scheme is an illegal investment model where participants are required to recruit others and rely on their investments to generate returns. In Multiverse, there is no such requirement for recruitment or financial dependency on other participants. Multiverse is a virtual world platform that offers users the opportunity to explore, create, and socialize in a digital environment.

You mentioned that land ownership in Multiverse is limited to those who joined early - anyone is allowed to purchase plots of land. We would like to clarify that land ownership in Multiverse is one aspect of the platform and is entirely optional. Users can enjoy Multiverse without owning land and still have access to a range of features and experiences within the app.

Regarding your concerns about the lack of activity, limited customization options, and the perceived imbalance in ownership, we acknowledge that Multiverse is a growing platform, and its development is an ongoing process. Our team is continuously working to improve and expand the platform, including adding new features, enhancing social interactions, and providing more opportunities for user engagement. We appreciate your feedback, and it will be shared with the development team to help guide future improvements.

We also want to emphasize that there is no purchase necessary to enjoy Multiverse. While there may be optional in-app purchases available, they are not required for a fulfilling experience within the app. Users can explore and interact with various worlds and creations for free, and even build out spaces within their personal metaverse for free, if so desired. 

At Multiverse, we strive to provide an engaging and enjoyable experience for our users. Your feedback is invaluable to us, and we appreciate your contribution in helping us make improvements. We remain committed to delivering a dynamic and inclusive virtual world platform.

Thank you for bringing your concerns to our attention. We encourage you to reach out to our customer support team at support@ftl.ltd if you have any additional concerns.

Sincerely, 
The Multiverse Team

reviews.review_title: No substance and lots of promises
reviews.review_description:
I have had this app since about a month after it launched. Originally I believed what I was being told and thought this could really go places and be something. Unfortunately it just seems to be a place to spend money (increasingly more by the month) with little or no return.

The metro stations were opened and promises of being able to move around a lot better... The extra stations are still not open and it's button bashing to get around. Max 12 people on at once and no control to meet up with who you want.


Sadly it's not one I continue to recommend as the ones who have bought in because I did so feel cheated.

Come back in 2 years, it might even be out of beta by then! 😒
